standard_description: Describe how words and phrases (e.g., regular beats, alliteration, rhymes, repeated lines) supply rhythm and meaning in a story, poem, or song.
subject: Reading

khan_exercises.title: The Moon: reading creative fiction; Milo and the Moon
khan_exercises.description: 2nd grade reading comprehension practice using creative fiction
topic_path: The Moon: reading creative fiction; Milo and the Moon

items.question_content:
Read the story, then answer the practice question.

___

##Milo and the Moon##



1. Late one night, Milo the Mouse sat on his windowsill. He stared at the moon. Its surface was full of craters. “That looks like cheese,” said Milo, who couldn’t see the moon very clearly. His stomach rumbled. He was awfully hungry! “I’m going to go to the moon and get some cheese,” he declared.

2. “Go to sleep, Milo,” said his mother.

![Milo dreaming he is riding on a bird's back to the moon.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/978823cb38c04ff2204b6151672b97d1.jpg)

3. So Milo went to sleep, and as he slept, he dreamed. Milo dreamed of flying to the moon on the back of a bird. Up and up they soared, until they landed on the moon. The moon was soft in parts, like cream cheese, and firm in parts, like cheddar, and even bouncy in parts, like mozzarella (and that part of the moon was Milo’s favorite). He ate and ate all night.

4. When Milo woke the next morning, he realized he had been chewing on his pillow, and not cheese. But he remembered his dream. He decided to go find a bird.

5. Milo left the house, and ran into his friend Tiny, the squirrel. “Where ya going?” Tiny asked.

6. “The moon!” Milo replied. “The moon is made of cheese, and I’m hungry.”

7. Tiny laughed. “The moon isn’t made of cheese! It’s rocky and rough. It only looks smooth from here.”

8. “We’ll see about that, Tiny! The next time you see me, I promise I’ll have cheese,” said Milo.

9. Milo climbed the tallest hill in the forest and waved until a bird landed in front of him. “Hello!” said Milo happily. “Would you take me to the moon, please?”

10. The bird, who was an owl, looked at the hill. Then she craned her head up high to look at the sky. “I think that’s further than I can fly, Milo!” she said. “But I do have a way to see the moon up close, without flying very far at all. It’s called a telescope.”

11. When Milo looked through the telescope, he got a really good look at the moon. “Oh!” said Milo. “It really isn’t made of cheese after all.”

12. “Nope,” said the owl. “It’s made of rocks.”

13. “Well, where am I going to get some cheese?” Milo cried as he realized his misunderstanding.

14. The owl moved the telescope with her wing, and Milo continued to look. “Oh my goodness,” he said. “A cheese store!”

15. The owl peeked through the telescope. “It looks like they’re having a sale on cheddar,” she said.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

___

##Practice question##

Read the sentence, and select the correct word from the dropdown menu.

**The author uses the word [[☃ dropdown 1]] to show that Milo thinks about his dream after he wakes up.**

choices.content: remembered
morning
flying
decided

options.explanation:

Read the story, then answer the practice question.

___

##Milo and the Moon##



1. Late one night, Milo the Mouse sat on his windowsill. He stared at the moon. Its surface was full of craters. “That looks like cheese,” said Milo, who couldn’t see the moon very clearly. His stomach rumbled. He was awfully hungry! “I’m going to go to the moon and get some cheese,” he declared.

2. “Go to sleep, Milo,” said his mother.

![Milo dreaming he is riding on a bird's back to the moon.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/978823cb38c04ff2204b6151672b97d1.jpg)

3. So Milo went to sleep, and as he slept, he dreamed. Milo dreamed of flying to the moon on the back of a bird. Up and up they soared, until they landed on the moon. The moon was soft in parts, like cream cheese, and firm in parts, like cheddar, and even bouncy in parts, like mozzarella (and that part of the moon was Milo’s favorite). He ate and ate all night.

4. When Milo woke the next morning, he realized he had been chewing on his pillow, and not cheese. But he remembered his dream. He decided to go find a bird.

5. Milo left the house, and ran into his friend Tiny, the squirrel. “Where ya going?” Tiny asked.

6. “The moon!” Milo replied. “The moon is made of cheese, and I’m hungry.”

7. Tiny laughed. “The moon isn’t made of cheese! It’s rocky and rough. It only looks smooth from here.”

8. “We’ll see about that, Tiny! The next time you see me, I promise I’ll have cheese,” said Milo.

9. Milo climbed the tallest hill in the forest and waved until a bird landed in front of him. “Hello!” said Milo happily. “Would you take me to the moon, please?”

10. The bird, who was an owl, looked at the hill. Then she craned her head up high to look at the sky. “I think that’s further than I can fly, Milo!” she said. “But I do have a way to see the moon up close, without flying very far at all. It’s called a telescope.”

11. When Milo looked through the telescope, he got a really good look at the moon. “Oh!” said Milo. “It really isn’t made of cheese after all.”

12. “Nope,” said the owl. “It’s made of rocks.”

13. “Well, where am I going to get some cheese?” Milo cried as he realized his misunderstanding.

14. The owl moved the telescope with her wing, and Milo continued to look. “Oh my goodness,” he said. “A cheese store!”

15. The owl peeked through the telescope. “It looks like they’re having a sale on cheddar,” she said.

[[☃ explanation 2]]

___

##Practice question##

**What is the lesson of the story?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Sometimes we think things that are not true, but our friends can help us.
Sometimes we eat more than is good for us, but our friends can help tell us to stop.
Always believe in yourself, even if no one else does.
Consider other people’s feelings before your own.

topic_path: Close reading: fiction; Little Red Riding Hood
Fairy Tales Retold: reading creative fiction; Little Red Riding Hood

items.question_content:
Read the story, then answer the practice question.

___

##Little Red Riding Hood##

1. Once there was a little girl with a cloak that had a red velvet hood. She was known as Little Red Riding Hood. One day her mother said, “Your granny is sick. Please take her some cakes; they will do her good. Remember to stay on the path, and do not stop along the way.” 

2. “Yes, mother,” said Little Red Riding Hood. Her granny lived in a house in the woods. When she entered the woods, she saw a wolf. The Wolf wished to eat Little Red Riding Hood for lunch. But she did not know that, so she was not afraid of him. 

![Little Red Riding Hood on the path through the woods talking to the Wolf.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e7664764168a5658ba0c68aa5d6c5512.png)
*Picture 1*

3. “And where are you going?” he asked.

4. “To my granny’s house,” said Little Red Riding Hood.

5. “And where does your granny live?” asked the Wolf.

6. “In the woods, a short distance from here, in a cottage under three big oak trees,” said Little Red Riding Hood. 

7. _What a tasty snack this girl would be,_ the Wolf thought, _but she’s not big enough for a meal. I must find a way to eat her and her granny too._

8. The Wolf said, “Why, look at all the pretty flowers. Why don’t you stop to rest and pick some of them?”

9. Little Red Riding Hood saw the lovely flowers, and said, “Granny would love some fresh flowers.” Forgetting what she promised her mother, she left the path and went into the woods to pick flowers. 

10. Each time she picked one, she saw others even prettier, and so she strayed deeper and deeper into the woods. 

11. The Wolf hurried to Granny’s cottage and knocked on the door.

12. “Who’s there?” said a little voice.

13. “It is I, Little Red Riding Hood,” said the Wolf.

14. “Oh, let yourself in, dear,” said Granny. 

15. The Wolf opened the door. Before Granny could realize what was happening, the Wolf gobbled her up!

16. Then the sly Wolf dressed in her nightgown. With a wicked grin, he got into the bed, and pulled up the covers. It was quite the disguise.

![The Wolf standing beside Granny's bed with a full belly, putting on her sleep bonnet.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8c937eb147fadf0a56d705cc4a5e7772.png)
*Picture 2*

17. Meanwhile, Little Red Riding Hood entered Granny’s house. She took a deep breath and called out, “Good morning, Granny.” But there was no answer. She went up to the bed. 

18. There she saw her granny, or so she thought. Little Red Riding Hood thought her granny looked very strange indeed.

19. “Oh, Granny,” she said, “what big ears you have!”

![Little Red Riding Hood looking at the Wolf tucked into bed, disguised as Granny.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8270556d572abd6674ca978d44411bed.png)
*Picture 3*


20. “The better to hear you with,” said the Wolf.

21. "Hmmm,” said Little Red Riding Hood. “Granny must be very sick, for her voice is much deeper than it used to be.”

22. “And Granny, what big eyes you have!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

23. “The better to see you with, my dear,” answered the Wolf. 

24. “And Granny, what big teeth you have!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

25. “The better to eat you with!” cried the Wolf as he sprang up and swallowed Little Red Riding Hood in one big gulp.

26. After his meal, the Wolf went to sleep, and began to snore. A hunter who was passing by the cottage heard the snoring. 

27. “My,” he said, “the old woman sounds terrible! I’d better check on her.”

28. The hunter saw the Wolf. He realized that the Wolf had eaten the old woman. He knew he had to set her free.

29. The hunter set them free, and out jumped Little Red Riding Hood and Granny.

![The hunter helping Little Red Riding Hood and Granny climb out of the Wolf's belly.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/22ff473b76f8f0aa470a5c18ffcbd07a.png)
*Picture 4*

30. “Oh, I’m so grateful you saved us!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

31. When the Wolf woke up, he ran away, never to be seen again.

32. And Little Red Riding Hood said to herself, “After this I shall always do as my mother tells me.” 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

___

##Practice Question##

**Choose whether each of the following is a character or a setting from the story.** 



[[☃ categorizer 1]]

##Little Red Riding Hood##

1. Once there was a little girl with a cloak that had a red velvet hood. She was known as Little Red Riding Hood. One day her mother said, “Your granny is sick. Please take her some cakes; they will do her good. Remember to stay on the path, and do not stop along the way.” 

2. “Yes, mother,” said Little Red Riding Hood. Her granny lived in a house in the woods. When she entered the woods, she saw a wolf. The Wolf wished to eat Little Red Riding Hood for lunch. But she did not know that, so she was not afraid of him. 

![Little Red Riding Hood on the path through the woods talking to the Wolf.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e7664764168a5658ba0c68aa5d6c5512.png)
*Picture 1*

3. “And where are you going?” he asked.

4. “To my granny’s house,” said Little Red Riding Hood.

5. “And where does your granny live?” asked the Wolf.

6. “In the woods, a short distance from here, in a cottage under three big oak trees,” said Little Red Riding Hood. 

7. _What a tasty snack this girl would be,_ the Wolf thought, _but she’s not big enough for a meal. I must find a way to eat her and her granny too._

8. The Wolf said, “Why, look at all the pretty flowers. Why don’t you stop to rest and pick some of them?”

9. Little Red Riding Hood saw the lovely flowers, and said, “Granny would love some fresh flowers.” Forgetting what she promised her mother, she left the path and went into the woods to pick flowers. 

10. Each time she picked one, she saw others even prettier, and so she strayed deeper and deeper into the woods. 

11. The Wolf hurried to Granny’s cottage and knocked on the door.

12. “Who’s there?” said a little voice.

13. “It is I, Little Red Riding Hood,” said the Wolf.

14. “Oh, let yourself in, dear,” said Granny. 

15. The Wolf opened the door. Before Granny could realize what was happening, the Wolf gobbled her up!

16. Then the sly Wolf dressed in her nightgown. With a wicked grin, he got into the bed, and pulled up the covers. It was quite the disguise.

![The Wolf standing beside Granny's bed with a full belly, putting on her sleep bonnet.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8c937eb147fadf0a56d705cc4a5e7772.png)
*Picture 2*

17. Meanwhile, Little Red Riding Hood entered Granny’s house. She took a deep breath and called out, “Good morning, Granny.” But there was no answer. She went up to the bed. 

18. There she saw her granny, or so she thought. Little Red Riding Hood thought her granny looked very strange indeed.

19. “Oh, Granny,” she said, “what big ears you have!”

![Little Red Riding Hood looking at the Wolf tucked into bed, disguised as Granny.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8270556d572abd6674ca978d44411bed.png)
*Picture 3*


20. “The better to hear you with,” said the Wolf.

21. "Hmmm,” said Little Red Riding Hood. “Granny must be very sick, for her voice is much deeper than it used to be.”

22. “And Granny, what big eyes you have!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

23. “The better to see you with, my dear,” answered the Wolf. 

24. “And Granny, what big teeth you have!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

25. “The better to eat you with!” cried the Wolf as he sprang up and swallowed Little Red Riding Hood in one big gulp.

26. After his meal, the Wolf went to sleep, and began to snore. A hunter who was passing by the cottage heard the snoring. 

27. “My,” he said, “the old woman sounds terrible! I’d better check on her.”

28. The hunter saw the Wolf. He realized that the Wolf had eaten the old woman. He knew he had to set her free.

29. The hunter set them free, and out jumped Little Red Riding Hood and Granny.

![The hunter helping Little Red Riding Hood and Granny climb out of the Wolf's belly.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/22ff473b76f8f0aa470a5c18ffcbd07a.png)
*Picture 4*

30. “Oh, I’m so grateful you saved us!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

31. When the Wolf woke up, he ran away, never to be seen again.

32. And Little Red Riding Hood said to herself, “After this I shall always do as my mother tells me.”

8. The Wolf said, “Why, look at all the pretty flowers. Why don’t you stop to rest and pick some of them?”

9. Little Red Riding Hood saw the lovely flowers, and said, “Granny would love some fresh flowers.” Forgetting what she promised her mother, she left the path and went into the woods to pick flowers. 

10. Each time she picked one, she saw others even prettier, and so she strayed deeper and deeper into the woods. 

11. The Wolf hurried to Granny’s cottage and knocked on the door.

12. “Who’s there?” said a little voice.

13. “It is I, Little Red Riding Hood,” said the Wolf.

14. “Oh, let yourself in, dear,” said Granny. 

15. The Wolf opened the door. Before Granny could realize what was happening, the Wolf gobbled her up!

16. Then the sly Wolf dressed in her nightgown. With a wicked grin, he got into the bed, and pulled up the covers. It was quite the disguise.

![The Wolf standing beside Granny's bed with a full belly, putting on her sleep bonnet.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8c937eb147fadf0a56d705cc4a5e7772.png)
*Picture 2*

17. Meanwhile, Little Red Riding Hood entered Granny’s house. She took a deep breath and called out, “Good morning, Granny.” But there was no answer. She went up to the bed. 

18. There she saw her granny, or so she thought. Little Red Riding Hood thought her granny looked very strange indeed.

19. “Oh, Granny,” she said, “what big ears you have!”

![Little Red Riding Hood looking at the Wolf tucked into bed, disguised as Granny.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8270556d572abd6674ca978d44411bed.png)
*Picture 3*


20. “The better to hear you with,” said the Wolf.

21. "Hmmm,” said Little Red Riding Hood. “Granny must be very sick, for her voice is much deeper than it used to be.”

22. “And Granny, what big eyes you have!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

23. “The better to see you with, my dear,” answered the Wolf. 

24. “And Granny, what big teeth you have!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

25. “The better to eat you with!” cried the Wolf as he sprang up and swallowed Little Red Riding Hood in one big gulp.

26. After his meal, the Wolf went to sleep, and began to snore. A hunter who was passing by the cottage heard the snoring. 

27. “My,” he said, “the old woman sounds terrible! I’d better check on her.”

28. The hunter saw the Wolf. He realized that the Wolf had eaten the old woman. He knew he had to set her free.

29. The hunter set them free, and out jumped Little Red Riding Hood and Granny.

![The hunter helping Little Red Riding Hood and Granny climb out of the Wolf's belly.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/22ff473b76f8f0aa470a5c18ffcbd07a.png)
*Picture 4*

30. “Oh, I’m so grateful you saved us!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

31. When the Wolf woke up, he ran away, never to be seen again.

32. And Little Red Riding Hood said to herself, “After this I shall always do as my mother tells me.” 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

___

##Practice Question##

**What is the central message of this story?**

topic_path: Rural, Suburban, Urban: reading creative fiction; The Little Yellow House

items.question_content:
Read the story, then answer the practice question.

___

##The Little Yellow House##

![An illustration of the Little Yellow House by a forest, with a duck pond in front.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9b4971d04e06377f486f521b2dbdd78f.jpg)
*Picture 1*

1. The Little Yellow House stood in a meadow surrounded by a thick stand of trees. Nearby, there were raspberry bushes and peach trees. There was a small pond with ducks that quacked happily all day long. Little Yellow House loved watching the squirrels scampering in search of food, and seeing the gopher burrowing underground. She loved watching the trees sway in the wind and drop colorful leaves in the fall. It was a lovely, peaceful location.

2. Little Yellow House also loved her family. The father and mother farmed the land. The little boy and little girl played in the yard and read books on the front porch. The family chatted around the dinner table every evening. Little Yellow House was very, very happy.

3. Years passed. Soon the children grew up and moved away. The trees of the forest slowly disappeared. Roads and homes started __popping up__ all around—but thankfully, there was not a house too close to her yet. 

4. One day a large truck came and dropped dirt into the pond. Soon the pond was nothing but a hole filled with dirt. The ducks flew away. Little Yellow House was very, very sad. 

![The Little Yellow House frowning while a dump truck does construction.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e6334330a03f905a3fc385d69f8927f3.jpg)
*Picture 2*

5. Not long after, Little Yellow House noticed a flurry of activity where the pond once stood. Soon another house appeared! Little Yellow House was not happy.

6. “How dare a house come so close to me!” she said to a squirrel. “Especially one that made my friends, the ducks, leave.” 

7. One morning Little Yellow House heard something that she never heard before. “Yoo hoo! Little Yellow House! My name is Little Blue House, and I am new to the neighborhood.”

8. Little Yellow House didn’t say anything. She was still mad. She didn’t like all this change. 

9. Soon more things began to change. A park was built with a small pond. The ducks came back! This made the Little Yellow House happier.

10. Then, more houses started to pop up. Children filled the streets and yards. They were playing games and having fun. Little Yellow House enjoyed watching the children laughing and smiling. Soon, she began to think to herself, “Maybe these new houses aren’t so bad. If there weren’t any new houses the children wouldn’t be here. I love to watch the children play.”

11. One spring morning, her new neighbors, Little Brown House and Little Green House, said, “Yoo hoo! Little Yellow House, don’t you just love to watch the children play? Don’t they look like they are having so much fun?”

12. Then it occurred to Little Yellow House that the other houses were trying to be friends. All she had ever known was the meadow, the forest, the animals, and the family. She remembered how the family talked every evening around the dinner table. She wanted someone to talk with about her day, too.

![The three houses smiling together while kids play in front.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9173d8db1beeff06223464356275d3ed.jpg)
*Picture 3*

13. Little Yellow House felt her heart open up. She replied, “Yes. I do love watching the children play. I also like to watch the ducks splash in the pond.”

14. “Me too!” said Little Brown House.

15. “The ducks are so much fun to watch!” said Little Green House. 

16. Little Yellow House smiled to herself. She was glad that she took a chance and spoke to Little Green House and Little Brown House. Now she had others to share her feelings with.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

___

##Practice Question##

**Why do you think the author used the phrase __popping up__ in paragraph 3 to describe the new houses?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

to show how long it took to build the new houses
to show how large and fancy the new houses were

##The Little Yellow House##

![An illustration of the Little Yellow House by a forest, with a duck pond in front.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9b4971d04e06377f486f521b2dbdd78f.jpg)
*Picture 1*

1. The Little Yellow House stood in a meadow surrounded by a thick stand of trees. Nearby, there were raspberry bushes and peach trees. There was a small pond with ducks that quacked happily all day long. Little Yellow House loved watching the squirrels scampering in search of food, and seeing the gopher burrowing underground. She loved watching the trees sway in the wind and drop colorful leaves in the fall. It was a lovely, peaceful location.

2. Little Yellow House also loved her family. The father and mother farmed the land. The little boy and little girl played in the yard and read books on the front porch. The family chatted around the dinner table every evening. Little Yellow House was very, very happy.

3. Years passed. Soon the children grew up and moved away. The trees of the forest slowly disappeared. Roads and homes started popping up all around—but thankfully, there was not a house too close to her yet. 

4. One day a large truck came and dropped dirt into the pond. Soon the pond was nothing but a hole filled with dirt. The ducks flew away. Little Yellow House was very, very sad. 

![The Little Yellow House frowning while a dump truck does construction.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e6334330a03f905a3fc385d69f8927f3.jpg)
*Picture 2*

5. Not long after, Little Yellow House noticed a flurry of activity where the pond once stood. Soon another house appeared! Little Yellow House was not happy.

6. “How dare a house come so close to me!” she said to a squirrel. “Especially one that made my friends, the ducks, leave.” 

7. One morning Little Yellow House heard something that she never heard before. “Yoo hoo! Little Yellow House! My name is Little Blue House, and I am new to the neighborhood.”

8. Little Yellow House didn’t say anything. She was still mad. She didn’t like all this change. 

9. Soon more things began to change. A park was built with a small pond. The ducks came back! This made the Little Yellow House happier.

10. Then, more houses started to pop up. Children filled the streets and yards. They were playing games and having fun. Little Yellow House enjoyed watching the children laughing and smiling. Soon, she began to think to herself, “Maybe these new houses aren’t so bad. If there weren’t any new houses the children wouldn’t be here. I love to watch the children play.”

11. One spring morning, her new neighbors, Little Brown House and Little Green House, said, “Yoo hoo! Little Yellow House, don’t you just love to watch the children play? Don’t they look like they are having so much fun?”

12. Then it occurred to Little Yellow House that the other houses were trying to be friends. All she had ever known was the meadow, the forest, the animals, and the family. She remembered how the family talked every evening around the dinner table. She wanted someone to talk with about her day, too.

![The three houses smiling together while kids play in front.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9173d8db1beeff06223464356275d3ed.jpg)
*Picture 3*

13. Little Yellow House felt her heart open up. She replied, “Yes. I do love watching the children play. I also like to watch the ducks splash in the pond.”

14. “Me too!” said Little Brown House.

15. “The ducks are so much fun to watch!” said Little Green House. 

16. Little Yellow House smiled to herself. She was glad that she took a chance and spoke to Little Green House and Little Brown House. Now she had others to share her feelings with.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Soon, she began to think to herself, “Maybe these new houses aren’t so bad. If there weren’t any new houses the children wouldn’t be here. I love to watch the children play.”

11. One spring morning, her new neighbors, Little Brown House and Little Green House, said, “Yoo hoo! Little Yellow House, don’t you just love to watch the children play? Don’t they look like they are having so much fun?”

12. Then it occurred to Little Yellow House that the other houses were trying to be friends. All she had ever known was the meadow, the forest, the animals, and the family. She remembered how the family talked every evening around the dinner table. She wanted someone to talk with about her day, too.

![The three houses smiling together while kids play in front.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9173d8db1beeff06223464356275d3ed.jpg)
*Picture 3*

13. Little Yellow House felt her heart open up. She replied, “Yes. I do love watching the children play. I also like to watch the ducks splash in the pond.”

14. “Me too!” said Little Brown House.

15. “The ducks are so much fun to watch!” said Little Green House. 

16. Little Yellow House smiled to herself. She was glad that she took a chance and spoke to Little Green House and Little Brown House. Now she had others to share her feelings with.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

___

##Practice Question##

**What is the story trying to teach us?**

choices.content: change can lead to happiness
nature should be protected
building houses is noisy
friends should spend time together

##Tom Thumb##

![Baby Tom Thumb in a tea cup, with his parents smiling down at him.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e2afa6f84be0c36fcde51703173f5f9d.png)
*Picture 1*

**Cast of Characters:**   
**Tom Thumb:** a very small boy, no bigger than an adult’s thumb  
**Randy:** a robber  
**Jed:** another robber

**Scene One:**  
(_Evening, in a barn outside the mayor’s house. TOM THUMB has just run away from a man that he was traveling with._)  
**Tom:** _(talking to AUDIENCE)_ Phew, what a day of adventure, eh friends? It was easy to escape from that silly man—because I’m so small. All I had to do was jump into a mousehole, and he couldn’t find me anywhere. I may be tiny, but I can still be tricky!
(_Sound of footsteps._)   
**Tom:** Someone’s coming—let’s hide!   
(_TOM hides. Enter TWO ROBBERS, JED and RANDY._)   
**Randy:** This is the house, Jed. The mayor’s traveled a long distance and won’t be back until tomorrow, so tonight is the perfect night to steal all his money.   
**Jed:**  But what about the cook and the maid? They’re certain to hear us breaking those famous iron bars around his money box.   
**Randy:** Then we’ll just have to do it very, very quietly, won’t we?   
**Tom:** (_to AUDIENCE_) Robbers! I like to play tricks, but stealing isn’t right. I feel another adventure coming on . . . watch this.   
(_TOM jumps out._)   
**Tom:** (_to ROBBERS_) Hello there!   
**Randy:** (_looking around_) Who said that?   
**Tom:** (_waving_) Down here!    
**Jed:** How curious. What a tiny fellow.

![Tom Thumb waving hello to Jed and Randy.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/aacbcbd9eaabf13a692e75a2c0200ee1.png)
*Picture 2*

**Tom:** Please take me with you into the house—I would love to help you.    
**Randy:** You? Ha! You’re very polite, but you’re rather small to be a robber.   
**Tom:** You said the mayor’s money is behind iron bars, and it will be noisy if you break them. But I’m so small, I can slip right between the bars without a sound.   
**Jed:** And why should we trust you?   
**Tom:** I may be tiny, but I’m a faithful fellow—and I love adventures!  
**Randy:** Alright then, tiny one. Just remember—everyone has to speak very quietly inside the house. Think you can do that?  
**Tom:** Oh, certainly!  
**Randy:** Very well. Now, let’s get robbing.

**Scene Two:**    
(_Inside the mayor’s house._)   
**Randy:** (_whispering_) Now then, tiny one. I expect climbing that table will be easy for you—then you just have to squeeze through the iron bars to get us the money. Ready?   
**Tom:** (_whispering_) Ready!   
**Randy:** (_whispering_) Remember: quiet as a mouse!   
(_TOM climbs up the table, and slips through the iron bars._)   
**Tom:** (_to AUDIENCE, whispering_) Now watch this! (_To ROBBERS, talking in his loudest voice_) HOW MUCH DO YOU WANT? SHOULD I JUST TAKE ALL OF IT?   
**Randy:** (_whispering_) SSSShhhhh! You’ll wake the cook and the maid—just give us the money!  
**Tom:** (_Even louder_) WHAT? YOU WANT ME TO TAKE ALL OF THE SEVERAL HUNDRED COINS OUT OF THIS MONEY BOX?   
(_Sound of a door banging upstairs, and people shouting and running._)   
**Jed:** (_whispering_) They’re coming, Randy!

![The robbers trying to hush Tom Thumb, as the maid comes down the stairs to investigate the noise.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8e1b492eb11abf800c8219488c70d3ff.png)
*Picture 3*

**Randy:** (_whispering_) You sneaky little rat-boy—you tricked us! Let’s get out of here, Jed.   
(_ROBBERS run away._)   
**Tom:** (_shouting after ROBBERS_) I may be tiny, but I can still be tricky!

[[☃ explanation 3]]

___

**Tom:** (_to AUDIENCE_) Robbers! I like to play tricks, but stealing isn’t right. I feel another adventure coming on . . . watch this.   
(_TOM jumps out._)   
**Tom:** (_to ROBBERS_) Hello there!   
**Randy:** (_looking around_) Who said that?   
**Tom:** (_waving_) Down here!    
**Jed:** How curious. What a tiny fellow.

![Tom Thumb waving hello to Jed and Randy.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/aacbcbd9eaabf13a692e75a2c0200ee1.png)
*Picture 2*

**Tom:** Please take me with you into the house—I would love to help you.    
**Randy:** You? Ha! You’re very polite, but you’re rather small to be a robber.   
**Tom:** You said the mayor’s money is behind iron bars, and it will be noisy if you break them. But I’m so small, I can slip right between the bars without a sound.   
**Jed:** And why should we trust you?   
**Tom:** I may be tiny, but I’m a faithful fellow—and I love adventures!  
**Randy:** Alright then, tiny one. Just remember—everyone has to speak very quietly inside the house. Think you can do that?  
**Tom:** Oh, certainly!  
**Randy:** Very well. Now, let’s get robbing.

**Scene Two:**    
(_Inside the mayor’s house._)   
**Randy:** (_whispering_) Now then, tiny one. I expect climbing that table will be easy for you—then you just have to squeeze through the iron bars to get us the money. Ready?   
**Tom:** (_whispering_) Ready!   
**Randy:** (_whispering_) Remember: quiet as a mouse!   
(_TOM climbs up the table, and slips through the iron bars._)   
**Tom:** (_to AUDIENCE, whispering_) Now watch this! (_To ROBBERS, talking in his loudest voice_) HOW MUCH DO YOU WANT? SHOULD I JUST TAKE ALL OF IT?   
**Randy:** (_whispering_) SSSShhhhh! You’ll wake the cook and the maid—just give us the money!  
**Tom:** (_Even louder_) WHAT? YOU WANT ME TO TAKE ALL OF THE SEVERAL HUNDRED COINS OUT OF THIS MONEY BOX?   
(_Sound of a door banging upstairs, and people shouting and running._)   
**Jed:** (_whispering_) They’re coming, Randy!

![The robbers trying to hush Tom Thumb, as the maid comes down the stairs to investigate the noise.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8e1b492eb11abf800c8219488c70d3ff.png)
*Picture 3*

**Randy:** (_whispering_) You sneaky little rat-boy—you tricked us! Let’s get out of here, Jed.   
(_ROBBERS run away._)   
**Tom:** (_shouting after ROBBERS_) I may be tiny, but I can still be tricky!

[[☃ explanation 3]]

___

##A Tiny Girl: Thumbelina## 

Once upon a time  
a tiny girl grew  
from out of a flower sparkling with dew.

![Thumbelina sitting in a red tulip.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f7ab7dcff50dc4fc79c987783a4b03ea.png)
*Picture 1*

She was kind, __gentle__,   
and lived without strife  
‘til a toad stole her as she slept in the night.  

The toad trapped the girl.  
She could not __escape__.   
How mean! How __wicked__! What a horrible fate! 

“Help me!” the girl cried.  
A fish heard her plea.   
He nibbled and nibbled to set the girl free. 

![Thumbelina kneeling on a lily pad, smiling down at the goldfish.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/573c30e45f46d1e23d60cbb859110f68.png)
*Picture 2*

Soon it was winter.   
The girl met a mouse.   
“Come stay with me in my warm winter house.” 

She sang for the mouse  
and all of her guests—  
including a mole who liked the girl best.   

“You will be my wife,”  
he ordered one night.     
“Oh no!” gasped the girl, shivering in fright.  

The girl did not wish  
a life underground.   
She fled on a bird. “I hope I’m not found!” 

But when she landed—  
Oh! What a surprise!    
A tiny young king was just the same size! 

![Thumbelina greeting the tiny king on his flower thrown.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/23eefda929bdb46790defb9d478cd353.png)
*Picture 3*

He ruled the land   
of those small and fair.   
And so Thumbelina lived happily there. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

___

##Tom Thumb##

![Baby Tom Thumb in a tea cup, with his parents smiling down at him.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e2afa6f84be0c36fcde51703173f5f9d.png)
*Picture 1*

**Cast of Characters:**   
**Tom Thumb:** a very small boy, no bigger than an adult’s thumb  
**Randy:** a robber  
**Jed:** another robber

**Scene One:**  
(_Evening, in a barn outside the mayor’s house. TOM THUMB has just run away from a man that he was traveling with._)  
**Tom:** _(talking to AUDIENCE)_ Phew, what a day of adventure, eh friends? It was easy to escape from that silly man—because I’m so small. All I had to do was jump into a mousehole, and he couldn’t find me anywhere. I may be tiny, but I can still be tricky!
(_Sound of footsteps._)   
**Tom:** Someone’s coming—let’s hide!   
(_TOM hides. Enter TWO ROBBERS, JED and RANDY._)   
**Randy:** This is the house, Jed. The mayor’s traveled a long distance and won’t be back until tomorrow, so tonight is the perfect night to steal all his money.   
**Jed:**  But what about the cook and the maid? They’re certain to hear us breaking those famous iron bars around his money box.   
**Randy:** Then we’ll just have to do it very, very quietly, won’t we?   
**Tom:** (_to AUDIENCE_) Robbers! I like to play tricks, but stealing isn’t right. I feel another adventure coming on . . . watch this.   
(_TOM jumps out._)   
**Tom:** (_to ROBBERS_) Hello there!   
**Randy:** (_looking around_) Who said that?   
**Tom:** (_waving_) Down here!    
**Jed:** How curious. What a tiny fellow.

![Tom Thumb waving hello to Jed and Randy.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/aacbcbd9eaabf13a692e75a2c0200ee1.png)
*Picture 2*

**Tom:** Please take me with you into the house—I would love to help you.    
**Randy:** You? Ha! You’re very polite, but you’re rather small to be a robber.   
**Tom:** You said the mayor’s money is behind iron bars, and it will be noisy if you break them. But I’m so small, I can slip right between the bars without a sound.   
**Jed:** And why should we trust you?   
**Tom:** I may be tiny, but I’m a faithful fellow—and I love adventures!  
**Randy:** Alright then, tiny one. Just remember—everyone has to speak very quietly inside the house. Think you can do that?  
**Tom:** Oh, certainly!  
**Randy:** Very well. Now, let’s get robbing.

**Scene Two:**    
(_Inside the mayor’s house._)   
**Randy:** (_whispering_) Now then, tiny one. I expect climbing that table will be easy for you—then you just have to squeeze through the iron bars to get us the money. Ready?   
**Tom:** (_whispering_) Ready!   
**Randy:** (_whispering_) Remember: quiet as a mouse!   
(_TOM climbs up the table, and slips through the iron bars._)   
**Tom:** (_to AUDIENCE, whispering_) Now watch this! (_To ROBBERS, talking in his loudest voice_) HOW MUCH DO YOU WANT? SHOULD I JUST TAKE ALL OF IT?   
**Randy:** (_whispering_) SSSShhhhh! You’ll wake the cook and the maid—just give us the money!  
**Tom:** (_Even louder_) WHAT? YOU WANT ME TO TAKE ALL OF THE SEVERAL HUNDRED COINS OUT OF THIS MONEY BOX?   
(_Sound of a door banging upstairs, and people shouting and running._)   
**Jed:** (_whispering_) They’re coming, Randy!

![The robbers trying to hush Tom Thumb, as the maid comes down the stairs to investigate the noise.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8e1b492eb11abf800c8219488c70d3ff.png)
*Picture 3*

**Randy:** (_whispering_) You sneaky little rat-boy—you tricked us! Let’s get out of here, Jed.   
(_ROBBERS run away._)   
**Tom:** (_shouting after ROBBERS_) I may be tiny, but I can still be tricky!


[[☃ explanation 4]]

*Picture 2*

Soon it was winter.   
The girl met a mouse.   
“Come stay with me in my warm winter house.” 

She sang for the mouse  
and all of her guests—  
including a mole who liked the girl best.   

“You will be my wife,”  
he ordered one night.     
“Oh no!” gasped the girl, shivering in fright.  

The girl did not wish  
a life underground.   
She fled on a bird. “I hope I’m not found!” 

But when she landed—  
Oh! What a surprise!    
A tiny young king was just the same size! 

![Thumbelina greeting the tiny king on his flower thrown.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/23eefda929bdb46790defb9d478cd353.png)
*Picture 3*

He ruled the land   
of those small and fair.   
And so Thumbelina lived happily there. 

[[☃ explanation 5]]

##Tom Thumb##

![Baby Tom Thumb in a tea cup, with his parents smiling down at him.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e2afa6f84be0c36fcde51703173f5f9d.png)
*Picture 1*

**Cast of Characters:**   
**Tom Thumb:** a very small boy, no bigger than an adult’s thumb  
**Randy:** a robber  
**Jed:** another robber

**Scene One:**  
(_Evening, in a barn outside the mayor’s house. TOM THUMB has just run away from a man that he was traveling with._)  
**Tom:** _(talking to AUDIENCE)_ Phew, what a day of adventure, eh friends? It was easy to escape from that silly man—because I’m so small. All I had to do was jump into a mousehole, and he couldn’t find me anywhere. I may be tiny, but I can still be tricky!
(_Sound of footsteps._)   
**Tom:** Someone’s coming—let’s hide!   
(_TOM hides. Enter TWO ROBBERS, JED and RANDY._)   
**Randy:** This is the house, Jed. The mayor’s traveled a long distance and won’t be back until tomorrow, so tonight is the perfect night to steal all his money.   
**Jed:**  But what about the cook and the maid? They’re certain to hear us breaking those famous iron bars around his money box.   
**Randy:** Then we’ll just have to do it very, very quietly, won’t we?   
**Tom:** (_to AUDIENCE_) Robbers! I like to play tricks, but stealing isn’t right. I feel another adventure coming on . . . watch this.   
(_TOM jumps out._)   
**Tom:** (_to ROBBERS_) Hello there!   
**Randy:** (_looking around_) Who said that?   
**Tom:** (_waving_) Down here!    
**Jed:** How curious. What a tiny fellow.

![Tom Thumb waving hello to Jed and Randy.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/aacbcbd9eaabf13a692e75a2c0200ee1.png)
*Picture 2*

**Tom:** Please take me with you into the house—I would love to help you.    
**Randy:** You? Ha! You’re very polite, but you’re rather small to be a robber.   
**Tom:** You said the mayor’s money is behind iron bars, and it will be noisy if you break them. But I’m so small, I can slip right between the bars without a sound.   
**Jed:** And why should we trust you?   
**Tom:** I may be tiny, but I’m a faithful fellow—and I love adventures!  
**Randy:** Alright then, tiny one. Just remember—everyone has to speak very quietly inside the house. Think you can do that?  
**Tom:** Oh, certainly!  
**Randy:** Very well. Now, let’s get robbing.

**Scene Two:**    
(_Inside the mayor’s house._)   
**Randy:** (_whispering_) Now then, tiny one. I expect climbing that table will be easy for you—then you just have to squeeze through the iron bars to get us the money. Ready?   
**Tom:** (_whispering_) Ready!   
**Randy:** (_whispering_) Remember: quiet as a mouse!   
(_TOM climbs up the table, and slips through the iron bars._)   
**Tom:** (_to AUDIENCE, whispering_) Now watch this! (_To ROBBERS, talking in his loudest voice_) HOW MUCH DO YOU WANT? SHOULD I JUST TAKE ALL OF IT?   
**Randy:** (_whispering_) SSSShhhhh! You’ll wake the cook and the maid—just give us the money!  
**Tom:** (_Even louder_) WHAT? YOU WANT ME TO TAKE ALL OF THE SEVERAL HUNDRED COINS OUT OF THIS MONEY BOX?   
(_Sound of a door banging upstairs, and people shouting and running._)   
**Jed:** (_whispering_) They’re coming, Randy!

![The robbers trying to hush Tom Thumb, as the maid comes down the stairs to investigate the noise.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8e1b492eb11abf800c8219488c70d3ff.png)
*Picture 3*

**Randy:** (_whispering_) You sneaky little rat-boy—you tricked us! Let’s get out of here, Jed.   
(_ROBBERS run away._)   
**Tom:** (_shouting after ROBBERS_) I may be tiny, but I can still be tricky!

[[☃ explanation 6]]

___

khan_exercises.title: The Moon: reading poetry; The Beautiful, Mysterious Moon
khan_exercises.description: 2nd grade reading comprehension practice using poetry
topic_path: The Moon: reading poetry; The Beautiful, Mysterious Moon

items.question_content:
Read the poem, then answer the practice question. 

___

##The Beautiful Mysterious Moon##

![Carlos reading a book and the poem's speaker smiling while the moon shines in the background.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3bff695453cf0a28e91008ba6f95726f.png)
 
I thought there was a rabbit inside the moon,     
One that nibbled on lettuce and __mushrooms__.    
But Carlos says that those are just craters.   
 
*That’s the way the moon works, the beautiful mysterious moon.*
 
I thought there was a giant in the sea    
That made the waves and pushed them onto the beach.    
But Carlos says that those are just tides.   
 
*That’s the way the moon works, the beautiful mysterious moon.*
 
I thought there was an old lady under the underground   
With a giant magnet that held us in place when we walk around.   
But Carlos says that it’s just gravity.   

*That’s the way the moon works, the beautiful mysterious moon.*

I don’t know if I agree or disagree,   
That the moon is responsible for tides and gravity.   
But Carlos read the science book, so we’ll have to believe   
That thing in the sky has an effect on you and me.   

[[☃ explanation 1]]

___

##Practice question##

**What TWO reasons explain why the poet uses the word __mushrooms__ in the second line of the poem?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.explanation: NP: Non-Prose

choices.content: because the *oo* sound in __mushroom__ is the same sound as the *oo* in “moon”
because the top of a mushroom is round, like a moon crater
because the moon tastes like __mushrooms__
because the *ush* sound in __mushroom__ rhymes  with the *uce* sound in  “lettuce”
because there are __mushrooms__ growing on the moon

khan_exercises.title: Rural, Suburban, Urban: reading realistic fiction; The City Cousin and the Country Cousin
khan_exercises.description: 2nd grade reading comprehension practice using realistic fiction
topic_path: Rural, Suburban, Urban: reading realistic fiction; The City Cousin and the Country Cousin

items.question_content:
Read the passage, then answer the practice question.

___

##The City Cousin and the Country Cousin##



Nicole lived in a big city. Her cousin, Charlie, lived on a farm. One day, Charlie went to visit Nicole. The cousins spent the day playing inside. Then they got dressed up to go see a play.

Charlie and Nicole walked to the train station. 
 
“Slow down,” said Charlie. “I can’t walk that fast in my fancy shoes!”

“We have to hurry, Charlie,” said Nicole. “Or we won’t catch the train on time. Then we’ll be late for the play!”

When they got on the train Charlie stuck his nose in his shirt.

“What are you doing?” asked Nicole, when she noticed Charlie’s nose stuck in his shirt. 

“It smells funny,” said Charlie. 

Nicole shrugged. “That’s just the way it smells here,” she said.

Charlie looked out the window. Building after building passed by so quickly, he didn’t get a chance to see what they were. It made him dizzy! People outside seemed to be rushing everywhere.

“It’s so busy in the city!” Charlie said. 

The idea that the city was busy had never really occurred to Nicole before. To her, it was just normal, everyday life. 

Charlie and Nicole had a great time at the play. But that night Charlie couldn’t get to sleep. 

“What’s wrong?” Nicole asked.

“I had fun in the city with you,” Charlie said. “But I miss my farm. The smells are different. The sounds are different there, too. I hear crickets instead of loud cars.”

“That sounds nice,” Nicole said.

“It is,” Charlie agreed. “Maybe you can come home with me tomorrow and check it out.”

So the next day, the cousins left the city for the country. 

“Are we almost there yet?” asked Nicole as she stared out the car window. 
“I haven’t seen anything for hours except for fields.”

Charlie’s mom smiled. “Yes,” she said. “We’ll be home in about 30 minutes.”

Nicole couldn’t believe it.  Thirty more minutes! How could anyone stand to stare at *nothing* for so long?  

When they finally arrived at the farm, three dogs came running up to the car. “Meet Ranger, Hank, and Miles,” said Charlie. 

“Oh!” said Nicole. “I’ve always wanted a dog, but living in a small apartment, it’s too hard to keep one.”

Nicole got out of the car, but was hit by a strong smell.  “What’s that horrible smell?” she asked.

Charlie just shrugged. “It’s just the way a farm with cows smells,” he said. 

Charlie’s mom smiled. “You get used to it,” she said. 

Nicole didn’t think she could ever get used to *that* smell.

But when Nicole saw the baby cows, her heart __melted__.

“They’re so cute!” she said. 

“Do you want to hold a baby chick?” asked Charlie. “They’re only a week old and super fuzzy.”

![Nicole happily holding baby chicks.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/619d7a8f3de10075607fcfc56e7b575e.jpg)

Nicole spent the rest of the day playing with the chicks . . . and by the end of the day, she *almost* didn’t notice the strong smell of the farm. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

___

##Practice Question##

Read the sentence from the story.

“But when Nicole saw the baby cows, her heart __melted__.”

**The author uses the word __melted__ to show that Nicole—**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: starts to see good things about the farm.
was feeling very warm.
wants to bring a baby cow to the city.
hopes to leave the farm soon.

##The City Cousin and the Country Cousin##



Nicole lived in a big city. Her cousin, Charlie, lived on a farm. One day, Charlie went to visit Nicole. The cousins spent the day playing inside. Then they got dressed up to go see a play.

Charlie and Nicole walked to the train station. 
 
“Slow down,” said Charlie. “I can’t walk that fast in my fancy shoes!”

“We have to hurry, Charlie,” said Nicole. “Or we won’t catch the train on time. Then we’ll be late for the play!”

When they got on the train Charlie stuck his nose in his shirt.

“What are you doing?” asked Nicole, when she noticed Charlie’s nose stuck in his shirt. 

“It smells funny,” said Charlie. 

Nicole shrugged. “That’s just the way it smells here,” she said.

Charlie looked out the window. Building after building passed by so quickly, he didn’t get a chance to see what they were. It made him dizzy! People outside seemed to be rushing everywhere.

“It’s so busy in the city!” Charlie said. 

The idea that the city was busy had never really occurred to Nicole before. To her, it was just normal, everyday life. 

Charlie and Nicole had a great time at the play. But that night Charlie couldn’t get to sleep. 

“What’s wrong?” Nicole asked.

“I had fun in the city with you,” Charlie said. “But I miss my farm. The smells are different. The sounds are different there, too. I hear crickets instead of loud cars.”

“That sounds nice,” Nicole said.

“It is,” Charlie agreed. “Maybe you can come home with me tomorrow and check it out.”

So the next day, the cousins left the city for the country. 

“Are we almost there yet?” asked Nicole as she stared out the car window. 
“I haven’t seen anything for hours except for fields.”

Charlie’s mom smiled. “Yes,” she said. “We’ll be home in about 30 minutes.”

Nicole couldn’t believe it.  Thirty more minutes! How could anyone stand to stare at *nothing* for so long?  

When they finally arrived at the farm, three dogs came running up to the car. “Meet Ranger, Hank, and Miles,” said Charlie. 

“Oh!” said Nicole. “I’ve always wanted a dog, but living in a small apartment, it’s too hard to keep one.”

Nicole got out of the car, but was hit by a strong smell.  “What’s that horrible smell?” she asked.

Charlie just shrugged. “It’s just the way a farm with cows smells,” he said. 

Charlie’s mom smiled. “You get used to it,” she said. 

Nicole didn’t think she could ever get used to *that* smell.

But when Nicole saw the baby cows, her heart melted.

“They’re so cute!” she said. 

“Do you want to hold a baby chick?” asked Charlie. “They’re only a week old and super fuzzy.”

![Nicole happily holding baby chicks.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/619d7a8f3de10075607fcfc56e7b575e.jpg)

Nicole spent the rest of the day playing with the chicks . . . and by the end of the day, she *almost* didn’t notice the strong smell of the farm. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

___

khan_exercises.title: Fairy Tales Retold: reading creative fiction; Granny the Hero
khan_exercises.description: 2nd grade reading comprehension practice using the text Granny the Hero
topic_path: Close reading: fiction; Granny the Hero
Fairy Tales Retold: reading creative fiction; Granny the Hero

items.question_content:
Read the passage, then answer the practice question.

___

##Granny, the Hero##

1. I’ll bet you’ve heard my story before. I’m the sweet old granny from Red Riding Hood. You know, the one who was gobbled up by a wicked wolf and rescued by a woodsman? I bet you think you know everything there is to know about this old tale. But you are so wrong!

2. Let’s start with that sly old Wolf. He thought he could fool a little old lady like me. I’m sure he thought he’d have an easy meal. I’m just a gentle old woman. I’m polite to everyone I meet. That’s why I invited him in. He thought he’d fooled me with his silly voice, but he hadn’t. That Wolf could never sound like my sweet Little Red. I would recognize her soft, gentle voice anywhere. I didn’t know what the Wolf was planning when I invited him in. But then, I’m sure he didn’t expect this old granny to be just as sly as he was.

3. The moment he swallowed me whole, I was already planning my escape. When you get to be as old as I am, you look forward to adventure. I knew if I put my mind to it, I’d be back out in no time.

4. The next thing I knew, there was my granddaughter, Little Red. The Wolf had swallowed her up too.

5. “Let’s play a game!” I said to Little Red. “Let’s see who can jump the highest.”

![Little Red and Granny jumping.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/36ae043eaecdad105e04a3b37a51c3f4.jpg)

6. Little Red and I started jumping. Oh, did we jump! We jumped and jumped and jumped. What a curious sight it must have been to see that old Wolf’s belly bouncing up and down! He howled and howled. The louder he howled, the harder we jumped. He finally opened his mouth, and out we popped! Then the Wolf ran away as quickly as he could—before we started jumping on top of him as well as inside him.

7. I was a bit startled as I looked around and saw a man I didn’t recognize. Little Red said he was a woodsman. He took one look at us and fell down to the floor with a thump! I guess seeing an old granny and a young girl pop out of a wolf’s mouth could be shocking. But then, the woodsman jumped up and declared himself a hero. He said he had rescued us. What nerve! He’d already run straight into town and told several people before I could stop him. I tried telling everyone the truth, but you know how it is. People just weren’t sure they could believe an old granny could beat a wicked wolf. But you know who does believe it? That old nasty wolf! We never saw him again.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

___

##Practice Question##

**Drag and drop the events into the gray box on the right, and put them in the correct order to create a summary.**

[[☃ orderer 1]]

options.explanation: Estimated 550L

correct_options: The Wolf swallows Granny and Little Red Riding Hood.
Granny and Little Red Riding Hood jump up and down to escape.
A woodsman tells everyone he saved Granny and Little Red Riding Hood.

items.question_content:
Read the passage, then answer the practice question.

___

##Granny, the Hero##

1. I’ll bet you’ve heard my story before. I’m the sweet old granny from Red Riding Hood. You know, the one who was gobbled up by a wicked wolf and rescued by a woodsman? I bet you think you know everything there is to know about this old tale. But you are so wrong!

2. Let’s start with that sly old Wolf. He thought he could fool a little old lady like me. I’m sure he thought he’d have an easy meal. I’m just a gentle old woman. I’m polite to everyone I meet. That’s why I invited him in. He thought he’d fooled me with his silly voice, but he hadn’t. That Wolf could never sound like my sweet Little Red. I would recognize her soft, gentle voice anywhere. I didn’t know what the Wolf was planning when I invited him in. But then, I’m sure he didn’t expect this old granny to be just as sly as he was.

3. The moment he swallowed me whole, I was already planning my escape. When you get to be as old as I am, you look forward to adventure. I knew if I put my mind to it, I’d be back out in no time.

4. The next thing I knew, there was my granddaughter, Little Red. The Wolf had swallowed her up too.

5. “Let’s play a game!” I said to Little Red. “Let’s see who can jump the highest.”

![Little Red and Granny jumping.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/36ae043eaecdad105e04a3b37a51c3f4.jpg)

6. Little Red and I started jumping. Oh, did we jump! We jumped and jumped and jumped. What a curious sight it must have been to see that old Wolf’s belly bouncing up and down! He howled and howled. The louder he howled, the harder we jumped. He finally opened his mouth, and out we popped! Then the Wolf ran away as quickly as he could—before we started jumping on top of him as well as inside him.

7. I was a bit startled as I looked around and saw a man I didn’t recognize. Little Red said he was a woodsman. He took one look at us and fell down to the floor with a thump! I guess seeing an old granny and a young girl pop out of a wolf’s mouth could be shocking. But then, the woodsman jumped up and declared himself a hero. He said he had rescued us. What nerve! He’d already run straight into town and told several people before I could stop him. I tried telling everyone the truth, but you know how it is. People just weren’t sure they could believe an old granny could beat a wicked wolf. But you know who does believe it? That old nasty wolf! We never saw him again.

[[☃ explanation 2]]

##Little Red Riding Hood##

1. Once there was a little girl with a cloak that had a red velvet hood. She was known as Little Red Riding Hood. One day her mother said, “Your granny is sick. Please take her some cakes; they will do her good. Remember to stay on the path, and do not stop along the way.” 

2. “Yes, mother,” said Little Red Riding Hood. Her granny lived in a house in the woods. When she entered the woods, she saw a wolf. The Wolf wished to eat Little Red Riding Hood for lunch. But she did not know that, so she was not afraid of him. 

![Little Red Riding Hood on the path through the woods talking to the Wolf.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e7664764168a5658ba0c68aa5d6c5512.png)

3. “And where are you going?” he asked.

4. “To my granny’s house,” said Little Red Riding Hood.

5. “And where does your granny live?” asked the Wolf.

6. “In the woods, a short distance from here, in a cottage under three big oak trees,” said Little Red Riding Hood. 

7. _What a tasty snack this girl would be,_ the Wolf thought, _but she’s not big enough for a meal. I must find a way to eat her and her granny too._

8. The Wolf said, “Why, look at all the pretty flowers. Why don’t you stop to rest and pick some of them?”

9. Little Red Riding Hood saw the lovely flowers, and said, “Granny would love some fresh flowers.” Forgetting what she promised her mother, she left the path and went into the woods to pick flowers. 

10. Each time she picked one, she saw others even prettier, and so she strayed deeper and deeper into the woods. 

11. The Wolf hurried to Granny’s cottage and knocked on the door.

12. “Who’s there?” said a little voice.

13. “It is I, Little Red Riding Hood,” said the Wolf.

14. “Oh, let yourself in, dear,” said Granny. 

15. The Wolf opened the door. Before Granny could realize what was happening, the Wolf gobbled her up!

16. Then the sly Wolf dressed in her nightgown. With a wicked grin, he got into the bed, and pulled up the covers. It was quite the disguise.

![The Wolf standing beside Granny's bed with a full belly, putting on her sleep bonnet.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8c937eb147fadf0a56d705cc4a5e7772.png)

17. Meanwhile, Little Red Riding Hood entered Granny’s house. She took a deep breath and called out, “Good morning, Granny.” But there was no answer. She went up to the bed. 

18. There she saw her granny, or so she thought. Little Red Riding Hood thought her granny looked very strange indeed.

19. “Oh, Granny,” she said, “what big ears you have!”

![Little Red Riding Hood looking at the Wolf tucked into bed, disguised as Granny.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8270556d572abd6674ca978d44411bed.png)

20. “The better to hear you with,” said the Wolf.

21. "Hmmm,” said Little Red Riding Hood. “Granny must be very sick, for her voice is much deeper than it used to be.”

22. “And Granny, what big eyes you have!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

23. “The better to see you with, my dear,” answered the Wolf. 

24. “And Granny, what big teeth you have!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

25. “The better to eat you with!” cried the Wolf as he sprang up and swallowed Little Red Riding Hood in one big gulp.

26. After his meal, the Wolf went to sleep, and began to snore. A hunter who was passing by the cottage heard the snoring. 

27. “My,” he said, “the old woman sounds terrible! I’d better check on her.”

28. The hunter saw the Wolf. He realized that the Wolf had eaten the old woman. He knew he had to set her free.

29. The hunter set them free, and out jumped Little Red Riding Hood and Granny.

![The hunter helping Little Red Riding Hood and Granny climb out of the Wolf's belly.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/22ff473b76f8f0aa470a5c18ffcbd07a.png)

30. “Oh, I’m so grateful you saved us!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

31. When the Wolf woke up, he ran away, never to be seen again.

32. And Little Red Riding Hood said to herself, “After this I shall always do as my mother tells me.” 

[[☃ explanation 2]]

Then the Wolf ran away as quickly as he could—before we started jumping on top of him as well as inside him.

7. I was a bit startled as I looked around and saw a man I didn’t recognize. Little Red said he was a woodsman. He took one look at us and fell down to the floor with a thump! I guess seeing an old granny and a young girl pop out of a wolf’s mouth could be shocking. But then, the woodsman jumped up and declared himself a hero. He said he had rescued us. What nerve! He’d already run straight into town and told several people before I could stop him. I tried telling everyone the truth, but you know how it is. People just weren’t sure they could believe an old granny could beat a wicked wolf. But you know who does believe it? That old nasty wolf! We never saw him again.

[[☃ explanation 4]]

___

##Practice Question##

Below is a list of events that happen in the two stories. Some events happen in just one of the stories, and some happen in both.

**For each event, mark whether it happens just in “Little Red Riding Hood,” just in “Granny, the Hero,” or if it happens in both stories.**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]

items: A little girl stops to pick flowers for her grandmother.
A wolf swallows a little girl and her grandmother.
A hunter saves a little girl and her grandmother.
A grandmother and a little girl jump up and down to escape.

items.question_content:
Read the two passages, then answer the practice question. 

___

##Little Red Riding Hood##

1. Once there was a little girl with a cloak that had a red velvet hood. She was known as Little Red Riding Hood. One day her mother said, “Your granny is sick. Please take her some cakes; they will do her good. Remember to stay on the path, and do not stop along the way.” 

2. “Yes, mother,” said Little Red Riding Hood. Her granny lived in a house in the woods. When she entered the woods, she saw a wolf. The Wolf wished to eat Little Red Riding Hood for lunch. But she did not know that, so she was not afraid of him. 

![Little Red Riding Hood on the path through the woods talking to the Wolf.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e7664764168a5658ba0c68aa5d6c5512.png)

3. “And where are you going?” he asked.

4. “To my granny’s house,” said Little Red Riding Hood.

5. “And where does your granny live?” asked the Wolf.

6. “In the woods, a short distance from here, in a cottage under three big oak trees,” said Little Red Riding Hood. 

7. _What a tasty snack this girl would be,_ the Wolf thought, _but she’s not big enough for a meal. I must find a way to eat her and her granny too._

8. The Wolf said, “Why, look at all the pretty flowers. Why don’t you stop to rest and pick some of them?”

9. Little Red Riding Hood saw the lovely flowers, and said, “Granny would love some fresh flowers.” Forgetting what she promised her mother, she left the path and went into the woods to pick flowers. 

10. Each time she picked one, she saw others even prettier, and so she strayed deeper and deeper into the woods. 

11. The Wolf hurried to Granny’s cottage and knocked on the door.

12. “Who’s there?” said a little voice.

13. “It is I, Little Red Riding Hood,” said the Wolf.

14. “Oh, let yourself in, dear,” said Granny. 

15. The Wolf opened the door. Before Granny could realize what was happening, the Wolf gobbled her up!

16. Then the sly Wolf dressed in her nightgown. With a wicked grin, he got into the bed, and pulled up the covers. It was quite the disguise.

![The Wolf standing beside Granny's bed with a full belly, putting on her sleep bonnet.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8c937eb147fadf0a56d705cc4a5e7772.png)

17. Meanwhile, Little Red Riding Hood entered Granny’s house. She took a deep breath and called out, “Good morning, Granny.” But there was no answer. She went up to the bed. 

18. There she saw her granny, or so she thought. Little Red Riding Hood thought her granny looked very strange indeed.

19. “Oh, Granny,” she said, “what big ears you have!”

![Little Red Riding Hood looking at the Wolf tucked into bed, disguised as Granny.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8270556d572abd6674ca978d44411bed.png)

20. “The better to hear you with,” said the Wolf.

21. "Hmmm,” said Little Red Riding Hood. “Granny must be very sick, for her voice is much deeper than it used to be.”

22. “And Granny, what big eyes you have!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

23. “The better to see you with, my dear,” answered the Wolf. 

24. “And Granny, what big teeth you have!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

25. “The better to eat you with!” cried the Wolf as he sprang up and swallowed Little Red Riding Hood in one big gulp.

26. After his meal, the Wolf went to sleep, and began to snore. A hunter who was passing by the cottage heard the snoring. 

27. “My,” he said, “the old woman sounds terrible! I’d better check on her.”

28. The hunter saw the Wolf. He realized that the Wolf had eaten the old woman. He knew he had to set her free.

29. The hunter set them free, and out jumped Little Red Riding Hood and Granny.

![The hunter helping Little Red Riding Hood and Granny climb out of the Wolf's belly.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/22ff473b76f8f0aa470a5c18ffcbd07a.png)

30. “Oh, I’m so grateful you saved us!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

31. When the Wolf woke up, he ran away, never to be seen again.

32. And Little Red Riding Hood said to herself, “After this I shall always do as my mother tells me.” 

[[☃ explanation 3]]

---

##Granny, the Hero##

1. I’ll bet you’ve heard my story before. I’m the sweet old granny from Red Riding Hood. You know, the one who was gobbled up by a wicked wolf and rescued by a woodsman? I bet you think you know everything there is to know about this old tale. But you are so wrong!

2. Let’s start with that sly old Wolf. He thought he could fool a little old lady like me. I’m sure he thought he’d have an easy meal. I’m just a gentle old woman. I’m polite to everyone I meet. That’s why I invited him in. He thought he’d fooled me with his silly voice, but he hadn’t. That Wolf could never sound like my sweet Little Red. I would recognize her soft, gentle voice anywhere. I didn’t know what the Wolf was planning when I invited him in. But then, I’m sure he didn’t expect this old granny to be just as sly as he was.

3. The moment he swallowed me whole, I was already planning my escape. When you get to be as old as I am, you look forward to adventure. I knew if I put my mind to it, I’d be back out in no time.

4. The next thing I knew, there was my granddaughter, Little Red. The Wolf had swallowed her up too.

5. “Let’s play a game!” I said to Little Red. “Let’s see who can jump the highest.”

![Little Red and Granny jumping.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/36ae043eaecdad105e04a3b37a51c3f4.jpg)

6. Little Red and I started jumping. Oh, did we jump! We jumped and jumped and jumped. What a curious sight it must have been to see that old Wolf’s belly bouncing up and down! He howled and howled. The louder he howled, the harder we jumped. He finally opened his mouth, and out we popped! Then the Wolf ran away as quickly as he could—before we started jumping on top of him as well as inside him.

7. I was a bit startled as I looked around and saw a man I didn’t recognize. Little Red said he was a woodsman. He took one look at us and fell down to the floor with a thump! I guess seeing an old granny and a young girl pop out of a wolf’s mouth could be shocking. But then, the woodsman jumped up and declared himself a hero. He said he had rescued us. What nerve! He’d already run straight into town and told several people before I could stop him. I tried telling everyone the truth, but you know how it is. People just weren’t sure they could believe an old granny could beat a wicked wolf. But you know who does believe it? That old nasty wolf! We never saw him again.

[[☃ explanation 4]]

___

##Practice Question##

Below is a list of events that happen in the two stories.

**Mark which events happen in "Little Red Riding Hood" and which happen in "Granny, the Hero."**




[[☃ categorizer 1]]

items: A girl points out the big eyes, nose, and teeth of her grandmother.
A wolf meets a girl in the woods.
A hunter saves a little girl and her grandmother.
A grandmother uses quick thinking to escape from a wolf.
A woodsman tells everyone he rescued a little girl and her grandmother.

##Little Red Riding Hood##

1. Once there was a little girl with a cloak that had a red velvet hood. She was known as Little Red Riding Hood. One day her mother said, “Your granny is sick. Please take her some cakes; they will do her good. Remember to stay on the path, and do not stop along the way.” 

2. “Yes, mother,” said Little Red Riding Hood. Her granny lived in a house in the woods. When she entered the woods, she saw a wolf. The Wolf wished to eat Little Red Riding Hood for lunch. But she did not know that, so she was not afraid of him. 

![Little Red Riding Hood on the path through the woods talking to the Wolf.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e7664764168a5658ba0c68aa5d6c5512.png)
*Picture 1*

3. “And where are you going?” he asked.

4. “To my granny’s house,” said Little Red Riding Hood.

5. “And where does your granny live?” asked the Wolf.

6. “In the woods, a short distance from here, in a cottage under three big oak trees,” said Little Red Riding Hood. 

7. _What a tasty snack this girl would be,_ the Wolf thought, _but she’s not big enough for a meal. I must find a way to eat her and her granny too._

8. The Wolf said, “Why, look at all the pretty flowers. Why don’t you stop to rest and pick some of them?”

9. Little Red Riding Hood saw the lovely flowers, and said, “Granny would love some fresh flowers.” Forgetting what she promised her mother, she left the path and went into the woods to pick flowers. 

10. Each time she picked one, she saw others even prettier, and so she strayed deeper and deeper into the woods. 

11. The Wolf hurried to Granny’s cottage and knocked on the door.

12. “Who’s there?” said a little voice.

13. “It is I, Little Red Riding Hood,” said the Wolf.

14. “Oh, let yourself in, dear,” said Granny. 

15. The Wolf opened the door. Before Granny could realize what was happening, the Wolf gobbled her up!

16. Then the sly Wolf dressed in her nightgown. With a wicked grin, he got into the bed, and pulled up the covers. It was quite the disguise.

![The Wolf standing beside Granny's bed with a full belly, putting on her sleep bonnet.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8c937eb147fadf0a56d705cc4a5e7772.png)
*Picture 2*

17. Meanwhile, Little Red Riding Hood entered Granny’s house. She took a deep breath and called out, “Good morning, Granny.” But there was no answer. She went up to the bed. 

18. There she saw her granny, or so she thought. Little Red Riding Hood thought her granny looked very strange indeed.

19. “Oh, Granny,” she said, “what big ears you have!”

![Little Red Riding Hood looking at the Wolf tucked into bed, disguised as Granny.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8270556d572abd6674ca978d44411bed.png)
*Picture 3*


20. “The better to hear you with,” said the Wolf.

21. "Hmmm,” said Little Red Riding Hood. “Granny must be very sick, for her voice is much deeper than it used to be.”

22. “And Granny, what big eyes you have!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

23. “The better to see you with, my dear,” answered the Wolf. 

24. “And Granny, what big teeth you have!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

25. “The better to eat you with!” cried the Wolf as he sprang up and swallowed Little Red Riding Hood in one big gulp.

26. After his meal, the Wolf went to sleep, and began to snore. A hunter who was passing by the cottage heard the snoring. 

27. “My,” he said, “the old woman sounds terrible! I’d better check on her.”

28. The hunter saw the Wolf. He realized that the Wolf had eaten the old woman. He knew he had to set her free.

29. The hunter set them free, and out jumped Little Red Riding Hood and Granny.

![The hunter helping Little Red Riding Hood and Granny climb out of the Wolf's belly.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/22ff473b76f8f0aa470a5c18ffcbd07a.png)
*Picture 4*

30. “Oh, I’m so grateful you saved us!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

31. When the Wolf woke up, he ran away, never to be seen again.

32. And Little Red Riding Hood said to herself, “After this I shall always do as my mother tells me.” 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

___

27. “My,” he said, “the old woman sounds terrible! I’d better check on her.”

28. The hunter saw the Wolf. He realized that the Wolf had eaten the old woman. He knew he had to set her free.

29. The hunter set them free, and out jumped Little Red Riding Hood and Granny.

![The hunter helping Little Red Riding Hood and Granny climb out of the Wolf's belly.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/22ff473b76f8f0aa470a5c18ffcbd07a.png)
*Picture 4*

30. “Oh, I’m so grateful you saved us!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

31. When the Wolf woke up, he ran away, never to be seen again.

32. And Little Red Riding Hood said to herself, “After this I shall always do as my mother tells me.” 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

##Little Red Riding Hood##

1. Once there was a little girl with a cloak that had a red velvet hood. She was known as Little Red Riding Hood. One day her mother said, “Your granny is sick. Please take her some cakes; they will do her good. Remember to stay on the path, and do not stop along the way.” 

2. “Yes, mother,” said Little Red Riding Hood. Her granny lived in a house in the woods. When she entered the woods, she saw a wolf. The Wolf wished to eat Little Red Riding Hood for lunch. But she did not know that, so she was not afraid of him. 

![Little Red Riding Hood on the path through the woods talking to the Wolf.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e7664764168a5658ba0c68aa5d6c5512.png)
*Picture 1*

3. “And where are you going?” he asked.

4. “To my granny’s house,” said Little Red Riding Hood.

5. “And where does your granny live?” asked the Wolf.

6. “In the woods, a short distance from here, in a cottage under three big oak trees,” said Little Red Riding Hood. 

7. _What a tasty snack this girl would be,_ the Wolf thought, _but she’s not big enough for a meal. I must find a way to eat her and her granny too._

8. The Wolf said, “Why, look at all the pretty flowers. Why don’t you stop to rest and pick some of them?”

9. Little Red Riding Hood saw the lovely flowers, and said, “Granny would love some fresh flowers.” Forgetting what she promised her mother, she left the path and went into the woods to pick flowers. 

10. Each time she picked one, she saw others even prettier, and so she strayed deeper and deeper into the woods. 

11. The Wolf hurried to Granny’s cottage and knocked on the door.

12. “Who’s there?” said a little voice.

13. “It is I, Little Red Riding Hood,” said the Wolf.

14. “Oh, let yourself in, dear,” said Granny. 

15. The Wolf opened the door. Before Granny could realize what was happening, the Wolf gobbled her up!

16. Then the sly Wolf dressed in her nightgown. With a wicked grin, he got into the bed, and pulled up the covers. It was quite the disguise.

![The Wolf standing beside Granny's bed with a full belly, putting on her sleep bonnet.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8c937eb147fadf0a56d705cc4a5e7772.png)
*Picture 2*

17. Meanwhile, Little Red Riding Hood entered Granny’s house. She took a deep breath and called out, “Good morning, Granny.” But there was no answer. She went up to the bed. 

18. There she saw her granny, or so she thought. Little Red Riding Hood thought her granny looked very strange indeed.

19. “Oh, Granny,” she said, “what big ears you have!”

![Little Red Riding Hood looking at the Wolf tucked into bed, disguised as Granny.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8270556d572abd6674ca978d44411bed.png)
*Picture 3*


20. “The better to hear you with,” said the Wolf.

21. "Hmmm,” said Little Red Riding Hood. “Granny must be very sick, for her voice is much deeper than it used to be.”

22. “And Granny, what big eyes you have!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

23. “The better to see you with, my dear,” answered the Wolf. 

24. “And Granny, what big teeth you have!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

25. “The better to eat you with!” cried the Wolf as he sprang up and swallowed Little Red Riding Hood in one big gulp.

26. After his meal, the Wolf went to sleep, and began to snore. A hunter who was passing by the cottage heard the snoring. 

27. “My,” he said, “the old woman sounds terrible! I’d better check on her.”

28. The hunter saw the Wolf. He realized that the Wolf had eaten the old woman. He knew he had to set her free.

29. The hunter set them free, and out jumped Little Red Riding Hood and Granny.

![The hunter helping Little Red Riding Hood and Granny climb out of the Wolf's belly.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/22ff473b76f8f0aa470a5c18ffcbd07a.png)
*Picture 4*

30. “Oh, I’m so grateful you saved us!” said Little Red Riding Hood.

31. When the Wolf woke up, he ran away, never to be seen again.

32. And Little Red Riding Hood said to herself, “After this I shall always do as my mother tells me.” 

[[☃ explanation 1]]
